channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: AI Agents Observability, Python Logging for OTel, & PySpark Code Linter
item.description: AI Agents Observability, Python Logging for OTel, & PySpark Code Linter Today's Highlights This week's highlights focus on critical tooling and observability for AI systems in production. We cover...

item.title: PostgreSQL: New Time-Series Extension & Replication Monitor; DuckDB in Production
item.description: PostgreSQL: New Time-Series Extension & Replication Monitor; DuckDB in Production Today's Highlights This week's highlights feature new PostgreSQL tools for time-series data and robust replication...

item.title: Intel Xe3P Leaks 160GB LPDDR5X; FlashAttention-2 in CuTe & Custom CUDA GPT-2 Engine
item.description: Intel Xe3P Leaks 160GB LPDDR5X; FlashAttention-2 in CuTe & Custom CUDA GPT-2 Engine Today's Highlights Intel's Xe3P "Crescent Island" GPU leaks reveal 160GB LPDDR5X VRAM, sidestepping HBM...

item.title: Turns AI development needs all the same things Human development does
item.description: For a while I've been "experimenting" with AI-assisted development; which meant opening a chat window, describing what I wanted, and hoping for the best. This is about what I built to make it more...

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Claude Design, & LLM Source Reliability Insights
item.description: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Claude Design, & LLM Source Reliability Insights Today's Highlights Google rolls out Gemini 3.5 Flash for faster, more cost-effective AI applications, while developers...

item.title: Local LLMs: Bytedance Lance 3B Multimodal, llama.cpp MTP, Ollama Client
item.description: Local LLMs: Bytedance Lance 3B Multimodal, llama.cpp MTP, Ollama Client Today's Highlights This week, Bytedance unveiled Lance, a 3B parameter open-source multimodal model accessible for consumer...

item.title: Can ClickHouse DELETE Data? A 2026 PR-by-PR Analysis
item.description: TL;DR ClickHouse has supported DELETE operations since 2018. As of 2026, it ships four production-grade deletion paths: heavyweight ALTER TABLE DELETE, lightweight DELETE FROM (default since...

item.title: GitHub Wasn't Hacked, But Your CI/CD Pipeline Might Be: Lessons from Grafana, CISA, and Shai-Hulud 2.0
item.description: GitHub wasn't hacked on May 19, 2026. GitHub.com is fully operational, all metrics green. But within the same news cycle, three incidents converged — and they all share the same wound: tokens,...

item.title: GitHub No Fue Hackeado, Pero Tu Pipeline Sí Podría Serlo: Lo Que Revelan Grafana, CISA y Shai-Hulud 2.0
item.description: El 19 de mayo de 2026 no hackearon GitHub. github.com está operativo, todas sus métricas en verde. Pero en el mismo ciclo de noticias coincidieron tres incidentes que comparten una herida...

item.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
item.description: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.

item.title: I Found a Coordinated GitHub Follow Botnet Hiding in My Followers
item.description: I've been building a personal analytics stack for my GitHub and DEV.to presence — traffic reports, bot audits, the works. While auditing my 97 GitHub followers today, I noticed something in the...

item.title: You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
item.description: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.

item.title: The future of Google is a search box that does everything
item.description: Last year, after watching Google's I/O keynote, I wrote that it felt like Google's future was Google googling. After watching this year's I/O keynote on Tuesday, I don't think Google just wants to...

item.title: How to use Google’s new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches
item.description: Google is launching AI-powered “information agents” that can monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes.

item.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
item.description: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, raised funding from Lightspeed Venture Partners.

item.title: Nintendo’s $500 Switch 2 bundle includes a game, and it’s available now
item.description: Nintendo recently teased the “Choose Your Game” Switch 2 console and digital game bundle, coming in early June. However, multiple retailers (including Nintendo itself) are already offering the...

item.title: Google’s AI future demands trust — and your personal data
item.description: Google has big promises for its AI-powered future - and a lot of it depends on your trust. At I/O 2026, Google described a bunch of new tools that it claims will make your life easier. Gemini...

item.title: Here are our favorite Memorial Day deals (so far)
item.description: Memorial Day marks the unofficial start of summer, and the holiday’s sales include plenty of deals on gadgets that can help you make the most of the season. If your plans involve beach days, pool...

item.title: Roundtables: Inside the Musk v. Altman Trial
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI, in which he alleged CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman had deceived him over the company’s non-profit status....

item.title: Democrats preview how they’d go after the Ticketmaster settlement if they regain power
item.description: A handful of Democrats called an unofficial hearing on Capitol Hill Monday to slam the Department of Justice's "trivial" and "pathetic" settlement with Live Nation-Ticketmaster, previewing how...

item.title: Elon Musk said Sam Altman “stole” a non-profit — but the trial showed he had similar aims
item.description: “By the end of this week, you and Sam will be the most hated men in America.”

item.title: Everything Announced at Google I/O 2026: Gemini, Search, Smart Glasses
item.description: Google is sprucing up its Gemini models, revamping search, and enabling AI agents in everything. There are also some spiffy new smart glasses coming this fall.

item.title: Ugreen’s new soccer ball-shaped tracker has up to 7 years of battery life
item.description: Ugreen has announced a new Apple Find My-compatible tracker with a novel design that limits where you can hide it, but greatly benefits battery life. Inspired by "major football tournaments" the...

item.title: Google takes a page out of Meta’s book, announces new audio-powered smart glasses
item.description: Google is calling the new devices "audio glasses," in that users will be able to issue verbal commands to them and get things done via its ecosystem of apps and services, including Gemini.

item.title: Meta Employees Are Scrambling to Use Up Benefits Ahead of Layoffs
item.description: On the eve of about 8,000 jobs being cut, employees are cashing in on headphone stipends and other perks while they still can.

item.title: OlmoEarth v1.1: A more efficient family of models
Kickstarter just killed its new mature content rules
item.description: Last week, we noted Kickstarter's new content guidelines, which had some pretty weird new additions, including a prohibition on "sexual wellness products that are not designed for insertion or...

item.title: In stunning display of stupid, secret CISA credentials found in public GitHub repo
item.description: SSH keys, plaintext passwords, other sensitive data had been up since November 2025.

item.title: Gemini will use Volvo’s external cameras to interpret parking signs
item.description: Gemini is gaining the power of sight and mobility. Today at the I/O conference, Google and Volvo announced that the AI-powered assistant will be able to access external cameras in the upcoming...

item.title: The 13 biggest announcements at Google I/O 2026
item.description: Google's I/O 2026 keynote today was once again full of AI-related announcements including a new family of Gemini 3.5 AI models, new features for Search and Gmail, and updates about its Project...
